Farn P orrrrcar- PRACTTcBs C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street . Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
March 28,2002
John Marshall Collins, P.C.
111 West St. John Street, Suite 400
San Jose, CA 951i3
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-02-067
Dear Mr. Collins
This letter is in response to your request for advice on the conflict-of-interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").l The Act requires the Commission to provide written
advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized
representative. (Section 831 1a@); regulation 18329(a) (ropy enclosed).) However, the
Commission may decline to provide written advice where the requestor is seeking advice that
requires us to evaluate conduct that has already occured. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) ) In
addition, the Commission may also decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate
or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F) )
Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the
requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83 I 14; regulation
18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written
advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending
investigation by the Enforcement Division. In addition, your current question involves analysis of
a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice at
this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective
administration of the Act so mandated by section 831 I 1.
Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request.
In addition, nothing in the Act prevents the official from seeking legal advice from you or private

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor sought written advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, but the specific details of the question are not provided in this declination letter.

CONCLUSION: The Commission declines to provide written advice at this time because the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division, and the question involves analysis of interrelated decisions, some of which have already occurred, such that issuing advice would relate to past conduct and would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide written advice to John Marshall Collins regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, citing a pending enforcement investigation on related matters and the involvement of past conduct in the analysis.
